sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 9.23 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.9)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finite group,$B$a block of characters of$G$,$D(B)$its defect group and$k(B)$(respectively,$k_0(B)$) the number of all irreducible complex characters (of height 0) lying in$B$. Conjectures: [r]{a)} (R.\,Brauer) \$k(B) |D(B)|$; this has been proved for$p$-soluble groups (D.\,Gluck, K.\,Magaard, U.\,Riese, P.\,Schmid, J.~Algebra, 279 (2004), 694--719); [r]{b)} (J.\,B.\,Olsson) \$k_0(B) |D(...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
